content:
URGENT PRODUCTION FIX — QR FLOW OPENS RAW API.QRSERVER IMAGE IN CHROME

The live Android flow leaves IVX and opens a raw QR image at:

api.qrserver.com/v1/...

This is incorrect. The QR image must be rendered inside IVX. It must never be opened automatically as a browser page.

Complete every item below.

1. Search the full repository for:

- api.qrserver.com
- qrserver
- qrImageUrl
- qr_image_url
- qrUrl
- QRCode
- Linking.openURL
- window.open
- router.push
- href
- WebView
- share QR
- payment QR
- login QR
- property QR
- referral QR

2. Identify every route, component, button, and service that can generate or open a QR code.

Return:

QR_IMPLEMENTATIONS_FOUND=
QR_SOURCE_FILES=
QR_TRIGGER_ROUTES=
QR_TRIGGER_BUTTONS=

3. Find the exact code that navigates to the raw QR image URL.

4. Remove all behavior equivalent to:

Linking.openURL(qrImageUrl)
window.open(qrImageUrl)
router.push(qrImageUrl)
href={qrImageUrl}

5. Separate the data model into:

qrImageUrl
destinationUrl
returnUrl
deepLinkUrl

6. Never use qrImageUrl for navigation.

7. Render the QR inside IVX using a modal or dedicated route.

Required UI:

- IVX header
- QR code
- Title
- Description
- Close button
- Android back support
- Copy link button
- Share button
- Loading state
- Error state
- Retry button

8. Prefer local QR generation inside React Native and web.

Generate the QR from destinationUrl using an approved QR library.

Do not rely on api.qrserver.com as the primary production solution.

9. If api.qrserver.com remains as fallback:

- Fetch only as an image
- Validate HTTP 200
- Validate image Content-Type
- Add timeout
- Add retry
- Never navigate to it
- Never expose sensitive payloads

10. Validate destinationUrl before generating the QR.

Reject:

- Empty values
- PLACEHOLDER
- UNKNOWN
- localhost
- 127.0.0.1
- Rork preview URLs
- Temporary Expo URLs
- Expired signed URLs
- Invalid protocols

11. Decode the generated QR during automated testing and prove the decoded value equals destinationUrl.

12. Add deep-link handling for IVX.

Verify Android and iOS can open the intended IVX route.

13. Preserve app state when the QR opens and closes.

14. Prevent repeated taps from opening multiple screens or browser tabs.

15. Add offline, timeout, invalid-data, and malformed-response handling.

16. Confirm the QR payload contains no:

- GitHub token
- Supabase key
- Session token
- Password
- API key
- Private owner data

17. Add automated tests for:

- QR displays inside IVX
- Chrome does not open automatically
- Android hardware back works
- Close button works
- Copy link works
- Share works
- Destination URL is correct
- QR decodes correctly
- Invalid URL is rejected
- Offline mode works
- Timeout works
- Double tap is blocked
- State survives background/foreground
- State survives app restart where applicable

18. Test every QR-enabled feature:

- Owner login
- Investor payment
- Property sharing
- Referral
- Wallet
- Authentication
- Checkout
- Any other QR flow discovered

19. Clear stale caches:

- Expo update cache
- AsyncStorage
- Browser cache
- Service worker
- CDN
- Web bundle cache

20. Commit, push, deploy, and verify production.

21. Verify on:

- Android physical device
- Android emulator
- iOS
- Mobile web
- Desktop web

22. Return production evidence only:

QR_ROOT_CAUSE=
QR_SOURCE_FILE=
QR_COMPONENT=
QR_TRIGGER_ROUTE=
QR_IMAGE_URL_NAVIGATION_REMOVED=YES/NO
QR_RENDERED_INSIDE_IVX=YES/NO
LOCAL_QR_GENERATION=YES/NO
EXTERNAL_BROWSER_AUTO_OPEN=YES/NO
DESTINATION_URL_VALIDATION=PASS/FAIL
QR_DECODE_MATCH=PASS/FAIL
ANDROID_BACK_QA=PASS/FAIL
OFFLINE_QA=PASS/FAIL
DOUBLE_TAP_QA=PASS/FAIL
ANDROID_QA=PASS/FAIL
IOS_QA=PASS/FAIL
WEB_QA=PASS/FAIL
COMMIT_SHA=
PUSH_STATUS=
DEPLOYMENT_OR_EXPO_UPDATE_ID=
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=
PRODUCTION_QA=
FINAL_STATUS=VERIFIED/UNVERIFIED

Do not return another local-only report.
Do not claim success until the live IVX app displays the QR internally and Chrome no longer opens automatically.
